inventoryPolicy.stockCaution: Purchased quantities are known, but remaining on-hand quantities should be verified before acting.
inventoryPolicy.reorderLogic: Use purchase history plus current tasks to surface buy-ahead reminders.

categories.label: Pre-emergent
categories.storeQuestion: What do I buy before crabgrass season?
categories.defaultTiming: Buy February-March, apply around the April redbud/full-bloom window if needed.

categories.label: Weed control
categories.storeQuestion: What do I use only when weeds justify it?
categories.defaultTiming: Spot or selective use only when label weather conditions are right.

categories.label: Fertilizer
categories.storeQuestion: What feeds the lawn without pushing the wrong season?
categories.defaultTiming: Light spring use only if justified; prioritize September and November.

categories.label: Seed
categories.storeQuestion: What tall fescue seed has been used before?
categories.defaultTiming: Plan late July-August, use mainly in September.

categories.label: Soil / topdressing
categories.defaultTiming: Buy only for a defined repair, leveling, or seed-support plan.

categories.label: Mulch
categories.storeQuestion: What mulch do I usually buy?
categories.defaultTiming: Review beds in spring; large refresh is likely every other year.

categories.label: Irrigation
categories.storeQuestion: What sprinkler supplies should I remember?
categories.defaultTiming: Add items here after startup, repair, or coverage checks.

categories.label: Consumables
categories.defaultTiming: Add blades, line, oil, filters, batteries, or similar parts as they become known.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Halts Crabgrass Preventer
items.typicalUse: Crabgrass prevention when prevention is planned.
items.spreaderSetting: Unknown; follow product label and record actual setting.
items.useTiming: April around redbud full bloom if crabgrass prevention is planned
items.notes: Water in according to label. Avoid heavy storm washout.
items.shortNote: Spring prevention bag; verify remaining stock before buying more.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Weed & Feed5
items.typicalUse: Conditional weed control plus feeding when weeds and label conditions justify it.
items.spreaderSetting: Unknown; follow label and record actual setting.
items.useTiming: Only when weeds and label conditions justify it
items.reorderTiming: Do not reorder automatically; decide based on actual weed pressure
items.notes: Avoid unnecessary fertilizer push, especially heading into heat.
items.shortNote: Do not treat as default fertilizer; use only with a reason.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Lawn Soil
items.typicalUse: Small repairs, leveling, and seed support where useful.
items.useTiming: Small repair areas, leveling, or seed support where useful
items.reorderTiming: Buy only for a defined repair or seed plan
items.notes: Do not treat bagged soil as a substitute for solving compaction or irrigation issues.
items.shortNote: Repair material, not a whole-yard fix.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Tall Fescue Mix
items.typicalUse: Tall fescue spot repairs and small thin areas.
items.useTiming: Best saved for September thin spots or small repairs
items.reorderTiming: Late July-August if fall seeding is planned
items.notes: Fall seeding is easier than spring/summer establishment around Valley Center, KS.
items.shortNote: Use mainly for fall work, not summer rescue.

items.name: Premium Dark Brown Mulch
items.reorderTiming: Review every other year or when beds visibly need refresh
items.notes: Large 2025 purchase supports an every-other-year planning assumption unless conditions change.
items.shortNote: Known mulch choice from the 2025 bulk refresh.
